description: Greek referendum on EU/ECB/IMF bailout terms. OXI (No) won 61.3% with 62.5% turnout. 8-day campaign. Tests extreme-compression dynamics with massive polling error.

key_events.description: Tsipras announces referendum in midnight TV address after Eurogroup breakdown.
Banks close, capital controls imposed, €60/day ATM limit. Initial panic benefits NAI, then anger shifts to OXI.
Greece misses IMF payment deadline. Crisis atmosphere cements 'dignity vs fear' framing.
Tsipras national TV address urging OXI 'for democracy and dignity'. OXI regains clear lead.
Juncker, Merkel, Hollande issue dire warnings about Grexit. Backfires as foreign intimidation.
Massive competing rallies in Athens. OXI rally significantly larger.

calibration_notes: Tests extreme-compression campaign (8 days) with very high volatility and massive polling error (polls underestimated OXI by 10+ points). Key: crisis-driven decision-making, rally-around-the-flag effect, capital controls/bank closures angered voters into OXI instead of scaring them to NAI, foreign pressure backfiring, enormous shy-OXI effect. Generational divide: youth overwhelmingly OXI.
